title: Kenyan Runner Makes History by Running a Marathon in Under Two Hours
summary: Sabastian Sawe, a runner from Kenya, made history by finishing the London Marathon in 1 hour, 59 minutes, and 30 seconds. This is the first time anyone has run a marathon in under two hours during a race. A marathon is a very long race, about 26 miles. Sawe ran faster than the old record of 2 hours and 35 seconds. Another runner, Yomif Kejelcha, also finished in under two hours, coming in second place. Sawe said he felt strong and happy. In the women's race, Tigst Assefa from Ethiopia also set a new world record. The London Marathon is a big event where runners from all over the world compete.

background_read: A marathon is a long-distance running race of 26.2 miles. The London Marathon is one of the most famous marathons in the world. Runners train very hard to prepare for it. The fastest runners can finish in a little over two hours.
Article_Structure: WHO: Sabastian Sawe, a Kenyan runner, made history by running the London Marathon in under two hours.
WHAT: He finished the race in 1 hour, 59 minutes, and 30 seconds, breaking the world record.
WHERE: The race took place in London, England.
WHY: Sawe wanted to break the world record and become the first person to run a marathon in under two hours in a race.

perspectives.description: Sabastian Sawe felt strong and happy after achieving his goal.
Fans were excited to see history being made.
